description: Compact fact-pattern fixtures for the remaining five template types. Each type gets at least one canonical scenario so the R5 validator regression catches a prompt tweak that accidentally drops a hard requirement. Extensible to 3/type once an operator grades the live-LLM output.

facts.children_details: One child, Aarav, 5 years old, in petitioner's custody.

facts.property_address: Flat 12A, Sunshine Apartments, Andheri West, Mumbai
facts.relief_sought: Remove the unauthorised partition erected on the common landing within fifteen days, failing which legal proceedings will follow.

statement_paragraphs: I am the counsel on record for the petitioner in WP No. 1234/2026.
The contents of the accompanying petition are true to my knowledge based on the records of the matter.
No facts material to the petition have been suppressed.

facts.narrative: Accused induced the complainant to invest INR 10,00,000 in a fictitious real-estate scheme. Accused forged the allotment letter and has since absconded.

facts.impugned_order_summary: The Sessions Court rejected the appellant's application for stay on the recovery proceedings, holding that no prima facie case for stay was made out and that the balance of convenience lay with the respondent revenue authority.
questions_of_law: Whether the Sessions Court erred in declining stay despite the demand exceeding the assessed liability by 38%.
Whether the balance of convenience requires deposit of the disputed component pending appeal.
facts.grounds_brief: 1. The Sessions Court overlooked the appellant's documented payment of the undisputed component of the demand. 2. The court mis-read Annexure D-2 (statutory acknowledgement). 3. The court applied the wrong test for stay under CPC Order XLI Rule 5 by demanding security for the entire demand rather than the disputed portion. 4. No reasoned finding was recorded on irreparable injury despite the appellant placing on record bank-statement evidence of liquidity strain.
facts.interim_relief_sought: Stay of recovery proceedings pending hearing of this appeal, conditional on deposit of the undisputed component already paid.
facts.record_references: Impugned order at pp. 14-19 of paper-book; Annexure D-2 at p. 47; bank statements at pp. 81-93.
